description_long: This object is a realistic representation of a crayfish or freshwater lobster, featuring a segmented, reddish-brown exoskeleton with darker mottled patterns along its back. The body is divided into a robust cephalothorax and a flexible, segmented abdomen, ending in a fan-shaped tail. Prominent claws (chelae) extend from the front, with one claw slightly larger than the other, and are accompanied by long, slender antennae projecting forward. Multiple walking legs are visible along the sides of the body, each jointed and covered in fine spines. The eyes are set on short stalks, positioned at the front of the cephalothorax, providing a wide field of vision. The surface texture appears hard and slightly rough, with a matte to semi-glossy finish typical of crustacean shells.
description: A detailed, reddish-brown crayfish with segmented body, large claws, long antennae, and a hard, textured exoskeleton.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the crayfish, highlighting the claws, antennae, and segmented tail.
description_view_1: Top-down view displaying the symmetrical arrangement of legs, claws, and the dorsal segmentation of the body.
description_view_2: Opposite side view emphasizing the other profile of the claws, legs, and the curvature of the tail.
description_view_3: Frontal view focusing on the claws, antennae, and the placement of the eyes on short stalks.
